title: Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia
chief_complaint: Term infant born with respiratory distress at delivery. Scaphoid abdomen. Breath sounds absent on left side. Heart sounds shifted to right. Diagnosed on prenatal ultrasound but parents declined termination.

physical_exam.Chest: Barrel-shaped chest. Absent breath sounds left hemithorax. Bowel sounds heard in left chest. Heart sounds shifted to right.
physical_exam.General: Severe respiratory distress. Cyanotic. Nasal flaring, grunting, retractions.

hidden_data.cxr: Left hemithorax filled with bowel loops and stomach. Mediastinal shift to right. Right lung compressed. ET tube in place. NG tube coiled in left chest (confirms stomach herniation).
hidden_data.abg: pH 6.95, PaCO2 90, PaO2 30, HCO3 10 (severe respiratory + metabolic acidosis)
hidden_data.echo: Right-to-left shunt via PDA and PFO (persistent fetal circulation). Pulmonary hypertension (RVSP > systemic BP). LV compressed.
hidden_data.prenatal: Diagnosed at 22 weeks. LHR (lung-to-head ratio) 0.8 (poor prognosis). Liver herniated into chest.

correct_diagnosis: Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia (CDH) - Left-Sided Bochdalek Hernia with Pulmonary Hypoplasia and Persistent Pulmonary Hypertension
key_learning_points: CDH = failure of diaphragm to close during fetal development → abdominal organs herniate into chest → compression of developing lungs → pulmonary hypoplasia + pulmonary hypertension
Most common: Left-sided Bochdalek hernia (85%). Right-sided (10%), Morgagni (5%). Associated with genetic syndromes in 30% (trisomies, Fryns syndrome)

Initial management: DO NOT BAG-MASK VENTILATE (inflates stomach/bowel in chest, worsens lung compression). Intubate immediately. NG tube for decompression. Gentle ventilation (permissive hypercapnia: PaCO2 45-65, pH >7.25). Target preductal SpO2 85-95%

Pulmonary hypertension management: Inhaled nitric oxide (iNO), Sildenafil, Milrinone. ECMO for refractory hypoxemia. Surgery: Delayed repair (after pulmonary hypertension stabilized) - reduces mortality vs immediate repair
Prognosis: Overall survival 70-80%. Poor prognostic factors: Liver herniation, low LHR on prenatal US, associated anomalies, need for ECMO
